briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Jul 17 Kimi K3 reignited AI capex doubts — SOX entered a bear market (−1.6%, −20.2% from Jun 22 high) and Nasdaq −1.4%; Brent settled $88.10 (+4.6%) as CENTCOM's seventh night of Iran strikes collided with Shanghai −3.05% (broke 3800) on WAIC day.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Jul 16 chip profit-taking dragged Nasdaq −1.47% and SOX −4.3%; Jul 17 A-shares plunged (Shanghai −3.05% to 3764, broke 3800) as global semi beta overwhelmed WAIC headlines; Logan/Jefferson Fed split keeps September hike debate alive with Brent $84.23.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: TSMC Q2 beat and raised FY26 revenue >40% confirmed AI demand, but H2 margin compression and BOK's first hike in 3.5 years sent Kospi −6.37%; Williams (Jul 15) said inflation peaked while Jul 15 CPI/PPI both missed and Brent settled $84.95.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Double disinflation miss — June CPI and PPI both cool vs consensus, Kospi +6.2% and ASML raised FY26 sales again; Warsh's Senate Day-2 kept the hawkish filter alive while Brent settled $85.90 and China Q2 GDP missed at 4.3%.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: CPI miss eases July-hike panic, but Warsh says not 'mission accomplished' — S&P +0.4% and Nasdaq +0.9% close up while Brent settles at $84.73 and Iran blockade risk persists.

briefings.title: Daily Financial Briefing
briefings.marketTone: Risk-off — Middle East escalation reprices oil, inflation, and Fed policy; semiconductors under pressure despite AI narrative.
